key_points: Transport and communication connect different regions and support economic development
Road transport provides door-to-door service and connects rural areas
Railways are important for transporting bulky goods over long distances
Pipeline transport is efficient for crude oil, petroleum products and natural gas
Mass communication helps spread information and awareness among people
examples: Border Roads Organisation builds roads in strategic areas
Kandla port was developed to reduce pressure on Mumbai port
Pipelines transport petroleum from oil fields to refineries
Air transport is useful in mountainous and remote areas
formulas: Favourable Balance of Trade occurs when Exports > Imports
exam_tips: Explain advantages and disadvantages of each mode of transport
Remember the importance of railways in the Indian economy
Understand the role of ports in international trade
Use examples while writing answers in exams
